mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods following a rate decline, or rolling Rank IC falls below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and imminent recession risk, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields negative 60-day returns in >50% of occurrences, or the interaction term loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) when tested against single-series baselines.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days specifically during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: The mechanism produces negative 20-day returns in >55% of recovery-phase observations, or out-of-sample directional accuracy drops below 52% in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly increases in real gross domestic product reflect sustained economic expansion and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of quarters following positive GDP revisions, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index annual growth rate surpasses 2.5%, markets anticipate aggressive monetary tightening that compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in >55% of threshold-crossing events, or the signal predictive accuracy is indistinguishable from random chance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative 20-day returns in >50% of triggered months, or the interaction effect fails to improve predictive IC over additive single-series models by at least 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals manufacturing contraction and weakening economic momentum, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of late-cycle contraction observations, or the signal directional accuracy falls below 48% in walk-forward testing.
